design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate mansion, defeat bosses guarding family members, solve environmental puzzles, collect power-ups and currency to unlock progression
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Non-linear exploration with hidden paths and optional content creating tension between thoroughness and forward momentum; rescue mechanic ties progression to boss defeats
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Gomez Addams rescuing the Addams Family from captivity by exploring a dangerous mansion filled with supernatural threats
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong commitment to non-linear open-ended exploration (Zelda/Metroid-like) combined with film license authenticity; puzzle-solving as core mechanic alongside combat; mansion design emphasizes secret discovery with invisible doors and hidden life caches

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives obtainable through hidden areas and power-ups; Pugsley's Den contains 5 1-UPs with additional secret area holding 27 lives; collectible throughout non-secret locations
difficulty.checkpointing: Door-to-door system acts as implicit checkpoint mechanism; power-ups persist across doors except fezi-copter which disintegrates upon room transition
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through optional secret areas, collectible hearts (up to 5 total), and power-up selection; non-linear progression allows players to tackle challenges in preferred order

progression.upgrade_system: Collectable power-ups (sword, golf club projectile weapon, fezi-copter flight, speed shoes); heart containers for health expansion; collectible potions in Game Boy version (Wolfman speed, Frankie invincibility, Sea Monster swim, Drac flight)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Rescue of family members triggers progression; defeat of bosses holding family hostage required; in some versions underground chambers require Lurch's music room activation after rescuing other family; NES version requires $1,000,000 collection for Morticia
replayability_hooks: Extensive hidden content (invisible doors, secret life caches with 27 lives in Pugsley's Den)
Multiple family member rescue order flexibility
Power-up experimentation and sequence breaking potential

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through mansion room design (freezing ice area for Wednesday, stove for Granny)
Non-player character guidance via Thing's clue boxes
Themed level design reflecting family members' locations and personalities
Integration of film plot (Abigail Craven, The Judge, Tully Alford antagonists) into gameplay objectives

boss_design.philosophy: Each boss guards and holds a specific family member hostage, creating narrative stakes for combat encounters; bosses serve as progression gates and story beats
notable_bosses: The Old Tree Boss (garden area, unused downward-facing animation frames suggest potential complexity)

faction_indicators: Addams Family members vs. Abigail Craven faction (antagonist and co-conspirators)
Uncle Fester (brainwashed ally, recovered through rescue)
danger_signals: Environmental hazards (swinging clock pendulums, fire lakes, stage hazards)
Kidnapping and captivity of family members signaling immediate threat
environmental_storytelling: Mansion layout organized around central Hall of Stairs with six accessible doorways
Specialized rooms (kitchen, games room, portrait gallery, music room) reflect family lifestyle
Hidden areas and secret passages emphasize mansion's mysterious nature
Themed rescue scenarios (freezing areas, stove traps) personalize each family member's danger

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - The Addams Family (SNES)
